2603.26859.title: Beyond Textual Knowledge-Leveraging Multimodal Knowledge Bases for Enhancing Vision-and-Language Navigation
2603.26859.paper_summary: Vision-and-Language Navigation (VLN) requires an agent to navigate through complex unseen environments based on natural language instructions. However, existing methods often struggle to effectively capture key semantic cues and accurately align them with visual observations. To address this limitation, we propose Beyond Textual Knowledge (BTK), a VLN framework that synergistically integrates environment-specific textual knowledge with generative image knowledge bases. BTK employs Qwen3-4B to extract goal-related phrases and utilizes Flux-Schnell to construct two large-scale image knowledge bases: R2R-GP and REVERIE-GP. Additionally, we leverage BLIP-2 to construct a large-scale textual knowledge base derived from panoramic views, providing environment-specific semantic cues. These multimodal knowledge bases are effectively integrated via the Goal-Aware Augmentor and Knowledge Augmentor, significantly enhancing semantic grounding and cross-modal alignment. Extensive experiments on the R2R dataset with 7,189 trajectories and the REVERIE dataset with 21,702 instructions demonstrate that BTK significantly outperforms existing baselines. On the test unseen splits of R2R and REVERIE, SR increased by 5% and 2.07% respectively, and SPL increased by 4% and 3.69% respectively. The source code is available at https://github.com/yds3/IPM-BTK/.
2603.26859.paper_summary_zh: 視覺與語言導航（VLN）要求代理根據自然語言指令在複雜的未見環境中導航。<lb/>然而，現有的方法通常難以有效捕捉關鍵的語義線索並準確地將其與視覺觀察對齊。<lb/>為了解決這一限制，我們提出了超越文本知識（BTK），這是一個VLN框架，協同整合了環境特定的文本知識與生成圖像知識庫。<lb/>BTK使用Qwen3-4B提取與目標相關的短語，並利用Flux-Schnell構建兩個大規模圖像知識庫：R2R-GP和REVERIE-GP。<lb/>此外，我們利用BLIP-2構建了一個基於全景視圖的大規模文本知識庫，提供環境特定的語義線索。<lb/>這些多模態知識庫通過目標感知增強器和知識增強器有效整合，顯著增強了語義基礎和跨模態對齊。<lb/>在R2R數據集上進行的廣泛實驗，涵蓋7,189條軌跡，以及REVERIE數據集上21,702條指令，證明BTK顯著超越了現有基準。<lb/>在R2R和REVERIE的測試未見分割中，SR分別增加了5%和2.07%，SPL分別增加了4%和3.69%。<lb/>源代碼可在https://github.com/yds3/IPM-BTK/獲得。
2603.26859.author: Dongsheng Yang et.al.
2603.26859.authors: Dongsheng Yang, Yinfeng Yu, Liejun Wang
